exam_price_notes: $93 exam fee + ~$30 test center fee. Fee waivers for military/low-income.
target_audience: Students who have completed two to three semesters of college French language study
recommended_experience: Knowledge and ability equivalent to that of students who have completed two to three semesters of college French language study

prerequisites.description: Two to three semesters of college French language study

retake_policy.notes: Each institution reserves the right to set its own credit-granting policy, which may differ from the American Council on Education (ACE). Contact your college to find out the score required for credit and the number of credit hours granted.

objectives.title: Listening comprehension: choosing the best responses to short spoken prompts
Listening comprehension: choosing the answers to questions based on longer spoken selections
Reading passages and authentic stimulus materials (reading comprehension)

official_study_resources.title: CLEP French Language Examination Guide
official_study_resources.notes: This guide provides practice questions for the CLEP French Language (Levels 1 and 2) Exam only.

official_study_resources.title: Study Resources: French Language
official_study_resources.notes: A study plan and list of online resources.

official_study_resources.title: Sample Questions: French Language: Levels 1 and 2
official_study_resources.notes: Answer sample questions related to the French Language exam.

official_study_resources.title: French Language Listening Exercise
official_study_resources.notes: Listen to sample questions in this listening section.

official_study_resources.title: What Your CLEP Score Means
official_study_resources.notes: Guide to understanding how CLEP scores are calculated and credit-granting recommendations for all exams.

official_study_resources.title: ACE Credit Recommendations
official_study_resources.notes: Recommendations for credit-granting scores from the American Council on Education.
